title: Post-endoscopy surgical follow-up

examination.text: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.
examination.rationale: Supports recovery, complication, information and follow-up documentation after sedation for post-endoscopy surgical follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy

examination.text: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.
examination.rationale: Supports documented recovery assessment and discharge instructions after sedation for post-endoscopy surgical follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 18–19 — suitability for discharge and instructions

follow_up.text: Supports documenting the endoscopic procedure context and indication or intended aim as known clinical-record information.
follow_up.rationale: Supports known endoscopic indication and procedure context for post-endoscopy surgical follow-up; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the endoscopic procedure context and indication or intended aim as known clinical-record information.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Procedure type and completeness, findings, biopsy or intervention, pathology, pain, bleeding, perforation, aspiration, diagnosis, medicine, surveillance, referral and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for post-endoscopy surgical follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
